description: Initialize Electron project with package.json, directory structure, and dependencies
steps: Create package.json with name 'esign', version '1.0.0', main 'main/main.js', scripts: { start: 'electron .', test: 'mocha test/test-sign.js --timeout 10000', package: 'electron-builder --config' }
Create directory structure: main/, main/lib/, renderer/, renderer/css/, renderer/js/, assets/fonts/, test/, test/fixtures/
Install dependencies: electron (as devDep), pdf-lib, @napi-rs/canvas, pdfjs-dist
Install dev dependencies: electron-builder, mocha, chai
Verify: node -e "const pkg = require('./package.json'); console.log(pkg.name === 'esign' && pkg.main === 'main/main.js' ? 'ok' : 'fail')"
Verify: ls main/lib renderer/css renderer/js assets/fonts test/fixtures

description: Download and bundle the 6 handwriting fonts
steps: Create a script assets/fonts/download-fonts.sh that downloads the 6 Google Fonts TTF files from the google/fonts GitHub repo raw URLs: DancingScript-Regular.ttf, GreatVibes-Regular.ttf, Caveat-Regular.ttf, Sacramento-Regular.ttf, Pacifico-Regular.ttf, HomemadeApple-Regular.ttf
Run the download script to fetch all 6 font files into assets/fonts/

Create main/lib/font-loader.js that exports a FONTS array with objects { name: string, label: string, path: string } for each font, and a loadFontBuffer(fontIndex) function that reads the .ttf as a Buffer. Use path.join(__dirname, '../../assets/fonts/') for the base path.
Verify: node -e "const fl = require('./main/lib/font-loader'); console.log(fl.FONTS.length === 6 ? 'ok' : 'fail')"
Verify: ls assets/fonts/*.ttf | wc -l
Create main/lib/signature-renderer.js that exports an async function renderSignature({ name, fontIndex, fontSize, color })

The function loads the font via font-loader, registers it with @napi-rs/canvas GlobalFonts.registerFromPath, creates a transparent canvas, measures text width, sets canvas dimensions (width from metrics + 20px padding, height = fontSize * 1.5), draws the text, and returns { dataUrl, width, height }
Default fontSize=48, default color='#000000'. dataUrl should be a data:image/png;base64 string.

Verify: node -e "const sr = require('./main/lib/signature-renderer'); sr.renderSignature({name:'Test User', fontIndex:0, fontSize:48, color:'#000000'}).then(r => console.log(r.width > 0 && r.dataUrl.startsWith('data:image/png') ? 'ok' : 'fail'))"

description: Build PDF signer module using pdf-lib
steps: Create main/lib/pdf-signer.js that exports an async function signPdf(pdfBytes, elements) returning a Uint8Array of the modified PDF

For each element with type='signature': the element will have a dataUrl property with the pre-rendered PNG. Decode the base64 PNG, call pdfDoc.embedPng(), draw the image on the correct page at (x, y, width, height) using drawImage. Coordinates use PDF bottom-left origin.

For each element with type='text': get the page, embed Helvetica font via pdfDoc.embedFont(StandardFonts.Helvetica), call page.drawText() at (x, y) with the specified fontSize and rgb color parsed from the hex color string.
Create test/fixtures/sample.pdf by writing a small script (test/fixtures/generate-pdfs.js) that uses pdf-lib to create a 1-page PDF with placeholder text and saves it. Run this script.

Verify: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const {signPdf} = require('./main/lib/pdf-signer'); const pdf = fs.readFileSync('./test/fixtures/sample.pdf'); signPdf(pdf, [{type:'text', page:0, x:100, y:700, width:200, height:20, value:'Hello', fontSize:12, color:'#000000'}]).then(buf => console.log(buf.length > 0 ? 'ok' : 'fail'))"

description: Create Electron main process entry point and preload script
steps: Create main/main.js: import electron (app, BrowserWindow, ipcMain, dialog), create a BrowserWindow with contextIsolation: true, nodeIntegration: false, preload pointing to main/preload.js, width 1200, height 800. Load renderer/index.html. Add a minimal application menu with File > Open PDF (triggers dialog) and File > Quit.

Create main/preload.js: use contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld to expose an 'electronAPI' object with methods: renderSignature(opts) → ipcRenderer.invoke('render-signature', opts), signPdf(pdfBytes, elements) → ipcRenderer.invoke('sign-pdf', pdfBytes, elements), openFileDialog() → ipcRenderer.invoke('open-file-dialog'), saveFile(bytes, defaultName) → ipcRenderer.invoke('save-file', bytes, defaultName)

Verify: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const m = fs.readFileSync('main/main.js','utf8'); const p = fs.readFileSync('main/preload.js','utf8'); console.log(m.includes('BrowserWindow') && p.includes('contextBridge') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Register IPC handlers for signature rendering, PDF signing, file dialogs
steps: Create main/ipc-handlers.js that exports a registerHandlers(mainWindow) function
Register ipcMain.handle('render-signature', ...) that calls renderSignature from signature-renderer.js and returns the result

Register ipcMain.handle('sign-pdf', ...) that calls signPdf from pdf-signer.js. For each signature element, render the signature first (call renderSignature), attach the dataUrl to the element, then pass all elements to signPdf. Return the Uint8Array result.

Register ipcMain.handle('open-file-dialog', ...) that opens dialog.showOpenDialog with filters for PDF files, reads the selected file, and returns { name: string, bytes: Uint8Array } or null if cancelled
Register ipcMain.handle('save-file', ...) that opens dialog.showSaveDialog with default filename, writes the bytes to the selected path, returns the saved path or null
Call registerHandlers(mainWindow) from main.js after window creation

Verify: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const content = fs.readFileSync('main/ipc-handlers.js','utf8'); console.log(content.includes('render-signature') && content.includes('sign-pdf') && content.includes('open-file-dialog') && content.includes('save-file') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Create the main HTML page with layout structure
steps: Create renderer/index.html with: a header bar showing 'esign' branding and an 'Open PDF' button, a drop zone area (visible when no PDF is loaded) with drag-and-drop support and a file picker button, a main workspace (hidden until PDF loaded) with a left sidebar for tools (signature panel + text panel) and a center scrollable area for the PDF viewer, a bottom action bar with 'Sign & Save' button (disabled until elements are placed)
Include <script> tags for the renderer JS files: pdf-viewer.js, placement.js, signature-panel.js, text-panel.js, app.js (app.js last since it initializes everything)
Include a <link> to css/style.css
Configure pdf.js workerSrc to point to the local pdfjs-dist worker file

Verify: test -f renderer/index.html && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('renderer/index.html','utf8'); console.log(c.includes('app.js') && c.includes('pdf-viewer.js') && c.includes('style.css') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Create CSS styles for the full application
steps: Create renderer/css/style.css with styles for: the header bar (fixed top, dark background), the drop zone (centered, dashed border, drag-hover highlight state), the workspace layout (sidebar 300px + viewer flex-1 using flexbox), the PDF page containers (.pdf-page with relative positioning), element overlays (absolute positioned, light border, cursor:move), resize handles (small square bottom-right corner), selected element highlight, the signature panel and text panel sections in the sidebar, the action bar (fixed bottom), loading spinner overlay, and toast notifications for errors
Use a clean modern design: #1a1a2e header, #f5f5f5 background, #4361ee accent color for buttons
Verify: test -f renderer/css/style.css && echo 'ok'

description: Implement PDF viewer with pdf.js rendering all pages
steps: Create renderer/js/pdf-viewer.js that exposes window.PdfViewer with methods: init(containerEl) to set the container, loadPdf(uint8Array) that uses pdfjsLib.getDocument to load the PDF and calls renderAllPages(), renderAllPages() that renders each page as a canvas inside a .pdf-page wrapper div (canvas + overlay div with class 'page-overlay'), getPageDimensions(pageIndex) returning {width, height} in PDF points, getScale() returning the current render scale factor, getPageCount() returning total pages
Render pages at a scale that fits the container width (max 800px). Each page wrapper should have data-page-index attribute.

Handle the pdf.js worker by setting pdfjsLib.GlobalWorkerOptions.workerSrc to the local pdfjs-dist worker path (use a require.resolve or relative path to node_modules/pdfjs-dist/build/pdf.worker.min.mjs)

Verify: test -f renderer/js/pdf-viewer.js && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('renderer/js/pdf-viewer.js','utf8'); console.log(c.includes('pdfjsLib') && c.includes('renderAllPages') && c.includes('getScale') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Implement placement engine for drag-and-drop elements on PDF pages
steps: Create renderer/js/placement.js that exposes window.Placement with methods: init() to set up state, addElement(pageIndex, type, options) that creates a draggable/resizable div on the page overlay at the specified page, removeElement(id) to delete an element, getElements() that returns all placed elements with positions converted from screen pixels to PDF points, clearAll() to remove everything

Each placed element div gets: a unique id, class 'placed-element', an X delete button in the top-right corner, a resize handle div in the bottom-right corner, and displays either an (for signatures) or an editable text span (for text elements)
Implement mouse-based drag: mousedown on element starts drag (track offset), mousemove updates element position via CSS left/top, mouseup ends drag. Constrain within the page overlay bounds.
Implement resize: mousedown on resize handle, mousemove updates element width/height, mouseup ends. Minimum size 30x15.
getElements() conversion: for each element, compute pdfX = elementLeft / scale, pdfY = (pageHeightPx - elementTop - elementHeight) / scale (flips Y axis for PDF bottom-left origin)

Verify: test -f renderer/js/placement.js && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('renderer/js/placement.js','utf8'); console.log(c.includes('addElement') && c.includes('getElements') && c.includes('removeElement') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Build signature creation panel in the sidebar
steps: Create renderer/js/signature-panel.js that exposes window.SignaturePanel with methods: init(containerEl) that builds the panel UI inside the given element

Panel UI contains: a text input for the signer's name, a grid of 6 clickable font preview boxes (each shows the typed name rendered via CSS @font-face using Google Fonts CDN URLs for preview — the actual signing uses server-rendered fonts), a font size slider (range 24-72, default 48), a color input (default #000000), a 'Preview' button that calls window.electronAPI.renderSignature() and displays the result in a preview area, and an 'Add to Document' button (enabled only after preview) that calls Placement.addElement(currentPageIndex, 'signature', { dataUrl, width, height, name, fontIndex, fontSize, color })
The font preview grid should update live as the user types their name

Verify: test -f renderer/js/signature-panel.js && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('renderer/js/signature-panel.js','utf8'); console.log(c.includes('renderSignature') && c.includes('fontIndex') && c.includes('addElement') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Build text field creation panel in the sidebar
steps: Create renderer/js/text-panel.js that exposes window.TextPanel with methods: init(containerEl) that builds the panel UI inside the given element

Panel UI contains: a text input for the field value (placeholder 'Enter date, name, etc.'), quick-insert buttons: 'Today (MM/DD/YYYY)' and 'Today (ISO)' that fill the input with the current date in the respective format, a font size selector (dropdown or number input, range 8-36, default 12), a color input (default #000000), and an 'Add to Document' button that calls Placement.addElement(currentPageIndex, 'text', { value, fontSize, color })

Verify: test -f renderer/js/text-panel.js && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('renderer/js/text-panel.js','utf8'); console.log(c.includes('addElement') && c.includes('text') && c.includes('fontSize') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Implement main app.js: file open, wire panels, sign & save flow
steps: Create renderer/js/app.js that initializes on DOMContentLoaded:

Set up file opening: 'Open PDF' button and header button both call window.electronAPI.openFileDialog(), receive { name, bytes }, convert bytes to Uint8Array, call PdfViewer.loadPdf(), hide drop zone, show workspace, store original PDF bytes and filename
Set up drag-and-drop on the drop zone: dragover/drop events, read dropped .pdf file as ArrayBuffer, same flow as above
Initialize SignaturePanel.init() and TextPanel.init() with their sidebar container elements
Initialize Placement.init()

Implement 'Sign & Save' button: collect elements via Placement.getElements(), call window.electronAPI.signPdf(originalPdfBytes, elements), receive signed bytes, call window.electronAPI.saveFile(signedBytes, 'signed-' + originalFilename)
Show a loading overlay during signing, show success/error toast messages

Verify: test -f renderer/js/app.js && node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('renderer/js/app.js','utf8'); console.log(c.includes('signPdf') && c.includes('openFileDialog') && c.includes('DOMContentLoaded') ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Configure Electron Forge / electron-builder for packaging
steps: Create forge.config.js (or update package.json build config) for electron-builder with: appId 'com.esign.app', productName 'esign', include assets/fonts/ in the asar bundle via files/extraResources config, targets for Windows (nsis), macOS (dmg), Linux (AppImage)
Ensure @napi-rs/canvas native bindings are handled correctly — add it to asarUnpack if needed so native .node files aren't broken by asar packing
Add a 'package' script to package.json: 'electron-builder' or 'electron-builder --config forge.config.js'

Verify: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const pkg = require('./package.json'); const hasConfig = fs.existsSync('forge.config.js') || pkg.build; console.log(hasConfig && pkg.scripts.package ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Add input validation and error handling throughout
steps: In main/ipc-handlers.js render-signature handler: validate name is non-empty string under 200 chars, fontIndex is integer 0-5, fontSize is number 8-200, color matches /^#[0-9a-fA-F]{6}$/. Throw descriptive errors on invalid input.

In main/ipc-handlers.js sign-pdf handler: validate pdfBytes is a Buffer/Uint8Array with length > 0 and < 50MB, elements is a non-empty array with length < 100. Validate each element has required fields.

In main/lib/pdf-signer.js: wrap pdf-lib operations in try/catch. If PDFDocument.load throws with 'encrypted' in the message, throw a user-friendly 'This PDF is encrypted/password-protected and cannot be signed' error.
In main/lib/pdf-signer.js: clamp page index to 0..pageCount-1, clamp x/y/width/height to page dimensions.
In renderer app.js: wrap all electronAPI calls in try/catch, show error toast to the user with the error message.

Verify: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const c = fs.readFileSync('main/ipc-handlers.js','utf8'); console.log(c.includes('validation') || (c.includes('throw') && c.includes('fontIndex')) ? 'ok' : 'fail')"

description: Handle multi-page PDFs and coordinate edge cases
steps: Add test/fixtures/generate-pdfs.js (or update existing) to also generate multipage.pdf — a 3-page PDF with pages of different sizes (Letter, A4, Legal) using pdf-lib. Run it.
In main/lib/pdf-signer.js: verify that signPdf correctly handles elements targeting different pages. Each element's page index should select the correct page from pdfDoc.getPages().
In renderer/js/pdf-viewer.js: ensure getPageDimensions(pageIndex) returns correct dimensions for each page individually (pages can have different sizes).
In renderer/js/placement.js: getElements() should use the correct page dimensions for each element's page when converting coordinates.

Verify: node -e "const fs = require('fs'); const {signPdf} = require('./main/lib/pdf-signer'); const pdf = fs.readFileSync('./test/fixtures/multipage.pdf'); signPdf(pdf, [{type:'text', page:0, x:100, y:100, width:200, height:20, value:'Page 1', fontSize:12, color:'#000000'}, {type:'text', page:2, x:100, y:100, width:200, height:20, value:'Page 3', fontSize:12, color:'#000000'}]).then(buf => console.log(buf.length > 0 ? 'ok' : 'fail'))"

description: Write unit/integration tests for backend modules
steps: Create test/test-sign.js using mocha and chai
Test signature-renderer: renderSignature with valid input returns object with dataUrl (starts with data:image/png), width > 0, height > 0
Test signature-renderer: renderSignature with empty name throws an error
Test signature-renderer: renderSignature with each fontIndex 0-5 succeeds
Test pdf-signer: signPdf with sample.pdf and a text element returns a valid PDF (starts with %PDF header bytes)
Test pdf-signer: signPdf with sample.pdf and a signature element (provide a pre-rendered dataUrl from signature-renderer) returns valid PDF
Test pdf-signer: signPdf with multipage.pdf and elements on different pages succeeds
Test pdf-signer: signPdf with out-of-range page index clamps without error
Verify: npx mocha test/test-sign.js --timeout 15000

description: Write end-to-end test script that exercises the full signing pipeline
steps: Create test/e2e-test.sh that: runs the PDF generation script to ensure fixtures exist, runs the mocha tests, uses Node.js to exercise the full pipeline (load sample.pdf → render a signature → sign the PDF with both a signature and text element → write output to test/fixtures/output-signed.pdf → verify output starts with %PDF bytes), cleans up the output file, exits 0 on success or 1 on failure
Make the script executable with chmod +x
Verify: bash test/e2e-test.sh

description: Final verification: all tests pass, all files exist, app starts
steps: Verify all source files exist: ls main/main.js main/preload.js main/ipc-handlers.js main/lib/pdf-signer.js main/lib/signature-renderer.js main/lib/font-loader.js renderer/index.html renderer/css/style.css renderer/js/app.js renderer/js/pdf-viewer.js renderer/js/placement.js renderer/js/signature-panel.js renderer/js/text-panel.js
Verify all 6 font files exist: ls assets/fonts/*.ttf | wc -l
Run unit tests: npx mocha test/test-sign.js --timeout 15000
Run e2e test: bash test/e2e-test.sh

Verify package.json is correct: node -e "const pkg = require('./package.json'); console.log(pkg.name === 'esign' && pkg.main === 'main/main.js' && pkg.scripts.start && pkg.scripts.package ? 'ok' : 'fail')"
Verify: npx mocha test/test-sign.js --timeout 15000 && bash test/e2e-test.sh && echo 'ALL CHECKS PASSED'
